problem_text: An economy has GDP = 560 billion dollars. Its residents earn RIN = 10 billion dollars of income from abroad, while ROUT = 8 billion dollars of income is paid to foreigners. Depreciation of capital over the year is DEP = 40 billion dollars. Compute the economy's net national product (NNP).

content.meaning: the value of capital worn out or used up during the year; it is subtracted from gross national product to obtain net national product, which is why net measures are smaller than gross measures

content.action: start from GDP and add net income earned from abroad, RIN minus ROUT, to obtain gross national product GNP
content.purpose: convert the domestic measure GDP into the national measure GNP before depreciation is removed

content.action: subtract depreciation DEP from gross national product GNP to obtain net national product NNP
content.purpose: remove the capital worn out over the year so the result reflects net rather than gross output
